script: Choose which main pipeline script to build. The classifier wrapper keeps the same bowl pipeline but swaps in the learned identity model.
load_env_watchdog: Load the current WATCHDOG_TAPO_COMMAND from .env and use it to prefill the builder.
use_venv_python: Use the repo's virtual environment Python executable instead of plain python.
use_video: Generate a command for local video replay by including --video.
use_url: Include an explicit RTSP URL with --url instead of relying on RTSP_URL from .env.
use_zone_polygon: Use --zone-polygon for a skewed bowl area. Turn this off to use the simpler --zone rectangle instead.
zone_edit: Open the mouse-based zone editor so you can drag the bowl area and press p to print updated coordinates.
headless: Run without an OpenCV preview window. Useful for replay and debug CSV runs.
loop_video: Loop the local replay video when it reaches the end.
no_snapshots: Disable snapshot image saving during the run.
save_clip_on_alert: Save a video clip when the alert condition is reached.
video: Path to a local video file for offline replay and tuning. This is only used when the video source toggle is on.
url: Explicit RTSP URL to use for the camera stream. Leave this off if RTSP_URL from .env should be used instead.
zone_polygon: Normalized bowl polygon in the form x1,y1;x2,y2;... . Best for perspective-skewed feeder zones.
zone: Normalized rectangle in the form x,y,w,h . Simpler than a polygon, but less flexible for angled camera views.
motion_threshold: Percentage of changed pixels inside the bowl zone needed to count as motion.
process_fps: How often frames are processed. Lower values reduce load, higher values react faster.
snapshot_cooldown: Minimum number of seconds between saved snapshots.
alert_seconds: How long the activity must persist before the alert fires.
clip_seconds: How long the saved alert clip should be after the trigger.
cat_model: YOLO model path used for cat detection, usually a local .pt model such as models\yolov8m.pt.
cat_confidence: Minimum confidence needed before a cat detection counts.
cat_enter_frames: How many consecutive cat-in-zone frames are required before cat presence starts.
cat_hold_seconds: How long cat presence stays active after brief missed detections.
possible_goblin_seconds: How long possible Goblin evidence must persist before logging a POSSIBLE_GOBLIN event.
cat_zone_overlap: Minimum fraction of the cat box that must overlap the bowl zone. Helps avoid edge-touch walk-bys.
cat_imgsz: Ultralytics inference image size. Larger values can improve detection but cost more GPU time.
identity_debug_csv: Optional CSV path for writing per-frame identity evidence during replay or tuning.
launch_origin: Label stored in APP_START so logs show whether the run was manual, watchdog-driven, or something else.
cat_detect_mode: When cat inference runs: always, only during motion, or auto based on source type.
cat_preprocess: Optional frame preprocessing for darker or IR-like scenes. Keep this off for normal lighting unless replay tests show it helps.
device: Inference device. Use cuda when GPU inference is available; otherwise use cpu.
id_goblin_support_conf: Minimum frame confidence before a frame counts toward Goblin support.
id_goblin_support_margin: Minimum Goblin-vs-Orange evidence margin required for a Goblin-support frame.
id_goblin_torso_white_min: Minimum torso-core white ratio needed before the frame can support Goblin.
id_goblin_periphery_margin_max: Maximum extra Goblin evidence allowed in the periphery before the frame is treated as background contamination.
id_lock_margin: Accumulator gap required to lock an unknown identity into Orange or Goblin.
id_switch_margin: Accumulator gap required to switch an already stable identity to the opposite class.
id_confirmed_goblin_support_count: How many recent Goblin-support frames are needed before confirmed Goblin hold can begin.
id_confirmed_goblin_hold_seconds: How long confirmed Goblin evidence must hold before the confirmed Goblin state becomes active.
extra_args: Raw extra command-line arguments appended at the end. Use this for uncommon flags not exposed in the form.
generated_command: This is the exact command string the builder will copy or save into .env.
refresh: Rebuild the command preview from the current form state.
copy_command: Copy the generated command to the clipboard.
write_to_env: Write the generated command into .env as WATCHDOG_TAPO_COMMAND.
